skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Creates detailed implementation plans for multi-step development work, including files, tests, commands, and handoff steps.
skill.summary: Turn requirements into precise, test-first implementation plans for engineering tasks.
skill.author: CodingCossack

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown instructions, inline code examples, and planning templates in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network access, credential access, prompt injection attempt, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static External Command Matches
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, or user-facing planning instructions. They do not execute commands inside the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is plain Markdown and the matched text appears inside documentation or examples. No script file or runtime command invocation exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are normal prose and Markdown template text. No hashing, encryption, key handling, or cryptographic API usage appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic behavior at the cited lines. The static hits are caused by harmless Markdown content, not weak algorithm use.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering requests often fail when work is vague or too large. This skill creates precise, test-first plans with files, commands, and handoff steps.
actual_capabilities: Breaks requirements into small engineering tasks with clear verification steps.
Defines exact files to create, modify, or test for each task.
Encourages a test-first workflow with expected failing and passing checks.
Produces plan documents with goals, architecture notes, and technology context.
Adds handoff guidance for subagent-driven or parallel execution workflows.
limitations: It does not inspect a repository unless the assistant has access to the project files.
It cannot guarantee commands will pass without current dependency and environment context.
It depends on clear requirements or a specification to produce useful plans.
It does not implement the planned code by itself.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Convert a feature request into ordered tasks, file changes, tests, and verification commands.

use_cases.title: Prepare Team Handoff
use_cases.description: Create a detailed plan that another developer or AI session can execute with minimal context.

use_cases.title: Structure Test-First Work
use_cases.description: Design a development path that starts with focused failing tests and ends with repeatable checks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feature Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short feature description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to create an implementation plan for this feature: [describe the feature]. Include files, tests, and verification commands.

prompt_templates.title: Plan From Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Requirements are available but need engineering structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to turn these requirements into a test-first implementation plan: [paste requirements]. Include assumptions and acceptance criteria coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Repository-Aware Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: The assistant can inspect a codebase before planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill. First inspect the repository patterns, then create a plan for [task]. Match existing structure and include exact file paths.

prompt_templates.title: Execution Handoff Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for another session or subagent workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to produce a handoff-ready plan for [project goal]. Include task boundaries, test commands, commit points, and execution mode options.

output_examples.input: Plan a new password reset flow for an existing web app.
output: A plan header with the goal, architecture, and technology context.
Tasks for tests, token generation, email flow, form handling, and verification.
A final handoff section that explains execution choices.

output_examples.input: Create a plan to add CSV export to an admin dashboard.
output: A sequence of small tasks covering UI controls, backend export logic, and tests.
Exact file targets and expected test results for each task.

best_practices: Provide the full specification, relevant constraints, and expected user behavior.
Let the assistant inspect existing files before asking for exact paths.
Ask for verification commands that match the project test tooling.
anti_patterns: Using the skill with only a vague goal and no acceptance criteria.
Skipping repository context when the plan must match existing patterns.
Treating the plan as a substitute for implementation review.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates implementation plans with small tasks, file paths, tests, commands, and handoff guidance.

faq.question: Does it write production code?
faq.answer: No. It plans the work and may include example code for the plan, but it does not execute the implementation.

faq.question: Can it work with an existing repository?
faq.answer: Yes, when the assistant can read the repository. It should inspect structure and patterns before writing the plan.

faq.question: Is it useful for test-driven development?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly favors writing failing tests before implementation and verifying each task.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What input produces the best result?
faq.answer: A clear feature request, acceptance criteria, technical constraints, and access to relevant project files.
